dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Go back, buy us a little food

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: The man solemnly warned us, saying, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you'

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Why did you treat me so badly by telling the man that you had another brother?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred, saying, 'Is your father still alive? Do you have another brother?' We told him in answer to these questions. Could we in any way know that he would say, 'Bring your brother down'?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: I will be a pledge of his safety; from my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame before you forever

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If we had not delayed, we could have gone and returned twice by now

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry a present down to the man, a little balm and a little honey, gum, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Take double the money with you. Carry back with you the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks. Perhaps it was an oversight

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Take also your brother, and arise, go back to the man

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, and may he send back your other brother and Benjamin. And as for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Oh, my lord, we came down the first time to buy food

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And when we came to the lodging place we opened our sacks, and there was each man's money in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; so we have brought it back with us

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And we have brought other money down with us to buy food; we do not know who put our money in our sacks

dialogue.speaker: josephs-steward
dialogue.englishText: Peace to you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has put treasure in your sacks for you; I received your money

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your servant our father is well; he is still alive

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: God be gracious to you, my son

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Serve the food
